name: Ramp
one_liner: Corporate card and spend-management platform whose Developer API exposes cards, transactions, bills, reimbursements, users, and accounting sync for agentic finance automation.
access_notes: Admins create a developer app in Settings > Developer to obtain client credentials; a self-serve sandbox at demo-api.ramp.com provides working test credentials with full sample data, no payment or approval needed to start. Access requires a Ramp account, and Ramp itself is a business product, but the API/sandbox path is self-serve. Certain elevated scopes (cards:read_vault for full PANs/CVVs) require PCI qualification via a support ticket, and partner/production MCP listing has extra review.
api_notes: Comprehensive REST API. Accounting alone has ~40 operations (GL accounts, custom fields, tax codes, vendors, codings, sync status); plus cards, transactions, bills (~8), reimbursements, users/departments/locations, banking, spend limits/programs, receipts, memos, statements, and financing applications. Well over 100 operations across categories. Batch endpoints support up to 500 items. OpenAPI spec published at /openapi/developer-api.json and machine-readable llms-*.txt docs available.
mcp_notes: Ramp ships an official open-source MCP server (github.com/ramp-public/ramp_mcp) and hosts a remote MCP at https://mcp.ramp.com/mcp plus a demo at https://demo-mcp.ramp.com/mcp. It provides a SQL interface over the Developer API for natural-language financial analytics; admins manage access via Company > Integrations > Ramp MCP.
main_blocker: None for core build: public REST API with self-serve credentials and sandbox. Only elevated card-vault (full PAN/CVV) access requires PCI qualification via support ticket.

evidence.claim: OAuth2 with authorization code and client credentials grants; access tokens last 10 days; sandbox API at demo-api.ramp.com; app created via Settings > Developer.
Scopes follow resource:permission; only Admin/Business Owner roles can grant OAuth2 permissions; broad resource coverage across accounting, bills, cards, transactions.
Ramp ships an official open-source MCP server and hosts remote MCP at mcp.ramp.com/mcp with a demo server.
Ramp built an MCP server providing a SQL interface over the Developer API for natural-language interaction with financial data.
Webhooks supported for events such as applications.status_updated, bills.*, and transactions.* with signature verification.

open_questions: Whether the free/starter Ramp tier grants developer-app access or only paid tiers; exact production go-live review requirements for third-party public apps beyond redirect-URI allowlisting.
